title: APIC Cluster Replication Latency
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_diag" earliest=-24h
| where repl_delay_ms>500 OR match(lower(message),"(?i)split|partition|lag")
| table _time, apic_id, repl_delay_ms, message
| sort -_time
description: Complements UC-18.1.7 with **database replication delay** and inter-APIC consensus metrics for split-brain prevention.
value: Complements UC-18.1.7 with **database replication delay** and inter-APIC consensus metrics for split-brain prevention.
implementation: Map fields from your APIC release; some metrics require Cisco DC Networking App. Alert on sustained replication lag.
visualization: Line chart (repl delay), Table (alerts), Single value (max lag ms).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: APIC `avictrl` / cluster diagnostics API.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Map fields from your APIC release; some metrics require Cisco DC Networking App. Alert on sustained replication lag.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_diag" earliest=-24h
| where repl_delay_ms>500 OR match(lower(message),"(?i)split|partition|lag")
| table _time, apic_id, repl_delay_ms, message
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**APIC Cluster Replication Latency** ‚Äî Complements UC-18.1.7 with **database replication delay** and inter-APIC consensus metrics for split-brain prevention.

Documented **Data sources**: APIC `avictrl` / cluster diagnostics API. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:cluster_diag. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_diag", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where repl_delay_ms>500 OR match(lower(message),"(?i)split|partition|lag")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **APIC Cluster Replication Latency**): table _time, apic_id, repl_delay_ms, message
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the APIC GUI for the same tenants, EPGs, and managed objects, and the affected fabric nodes.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (repl delay), Table (alerts), Single value (max lag ms).
grandmaExplanation: Complements UC-18.1.7 with **database replication delay** and inter-APIC consensus metrics for split-brain prevention ‚Äî so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: Controller failovers during planned upgrades, scheduled HA tests, or after restart of management nodes.
